domain: Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Space (RKHS) / Mercer's theorem
specific_mechanism: RKHS-BG: Mercer Modal-Energy Bandwidth Gate. Over a calibration sample of context tokens, compute the empirical Gram matrix G_ij = exp(<x_i,x_j>/√d). Compute top-r Mercer modes (λ_i, φ_i). Modal energy ratio E_r(x) = (Σ_{i≤r} λ_i φ_i(x)²)/(Σ_i λ_i φ_i(x)²) is computed per-token. Per-token softmax temperature τ_t = τ_0 / (1 + α E_r(x_t)) sharpens attention for high-modal-energy tokens.
llm_application: RKHS-BG context-gating: at training start, fit top-r Mercer modes of the kernel matrix on a calibration prompt batch. At each forward pass, compute per-token modal energy E_r. Adapt the per-token softmax temperature τ_t for attention so high-E_r tokens get sharper attention. The intent: tokens in well-approximated low-rank regions get sharp, peaked attention; high-rank (off-manifold) tokens get diffuse attention.
novelty_claim: Hierarchical Kernel Transformer (2604.08829) and Sparse Attention as Compact Kernel Regression (2601.22766) are adjacent. The Mercer modal-energy specifically as a per-token bandwidth gate is less common.
motivation_justification: Attention is kernel regression; Mercer's theorem gives the eigendecomposition. The candidate transfers the modal-energy ratio directly as a temperature controller.
sub_mechanisms: M_1: Empirical Gram matrix over calibration batch
M_3: Per-token modal energy ratio E_r
content_words: rkhs reproducing kernel hilbert space mercer
empirical gram matrix calibration sample top-r
per-token diffuse vs sharp attention adapter

llm_side: per-token diffuse vs sharp attention adapter
